Narrative_Meaning: Jokers signify creativity, humour and surprises, both good and bad.

Yarn.Yarn_Description: The Beyond card represents that which is outside. In many ways it is the opposite of the World, as that card implies a wholeness and completeness, the Beyond tells us that even the Omniverse has its impossibilities. The Beyond can directly represent unknown factors, or the factors from beyond creation that are called the Increated. The Beyond can also represent the Supernatural, as anything beyond nature is within the bounds of the Beyond.

Significator.Plot: The Beyond Plot is often a tale of horror (usually cosmic), as some force beyond normal is unleashed upon the world. Demons, Undead and other tools of the Increated are common to Beyond Plots. Technically, any unknown can be a part of the Beyond, extraterrestrials, ghosts, spirits, a stranger from out of town, they can all be part of a Beyond tale. The only important factor is that it is Beyond normal in some way.
Significator.Side: A Side marked by the Beyond is usually touched by some other world. This can range from their Embodiments just being considered a bit weird to actually being possessed by the Increated. When the External side or Shadows side is marked by The Beyond then the Embodiments can actually be Aliens, Fantastic races or Increated, Goblins, Demons or Demon-Lords as required.
Significator.Character:
The Beyond Significator indicates a Character who is an outsider, alien or weirdo.

They Gain Yin when they speak or act strangely, or ask odd questions.

They Gain Yang when they answer a question, especially if it wasn't directed at them, or the answer is weird.

They Gain Chi when they act as if something common or normal is unusual or funny, or they act as though they have forgotten (or never knew the meaning of) a common word.

They also gain +1 Success Level to any strange, weird or "impossible" action.
Significator.Scene: The Beyond Scene is usually used to represent any scene that goes beyond the normal risks or limits of the Plot. This Scene may be at much Higher Stakes than any other, or it may involve forces that have not appeared in this story, and actually represents the Frame of a second, larger Story impinging on the first. Yarn-Tellers that play a Beyond card can narrate almost anything following it. The Referee and other Yarn-Tellers should try to roll with this Narration as much as possible. Narration during a Beyond may not directly contradict any previous Narration, or it runs the risk of becoming Unreliable Narration, which in turn can be ignored by later Yarn-Tellers.

Yarn.Aspect_Description: The Hook targets the Character's Hitches, the bigger the better.
Yarn.Hook_Description: The Hook is just one of those out there crazy starts to a story. Perhaps they come around on a beach near a crashed plane, or wrecked boat, with no memories of where they were going or why.
Yarn.Fray_Description: A holdover from Old School Gaming. The encounter is against a random Monster (Force of Nature Extra or similar powerful individual) or a group of similar powered Characters (Cast Extras) or a large group (2 to 1 outnumbering) Chorus (Extras) or a tide of Vex Extras.
Yarn.Snag_Description: A Triple Threat Draws and Plays an additional 2 Cards one as a Second Fray Ordeal and one as a Snag

NPC.Description: Undead come in lots of different types, but you'd be surprised how many can be modelled with this Archetype.

Yarn.Sweeping_Text: The Sweeping is an Interlogue, where we cut away from the Pressed Embodiment to find out what is happening to some other Characters usually in a different Plot, but it can be a Scene or Two for the Dominant Embodiment.
Yarn.History_Description: The Beyond indicates that the events that have led to the current situation were not natural in nature, some supernatural, preternatural, spiritual, divine, mystical, spectral, magical, occult or other-worldly force (often the Increated or Fæ) are involved.
Yarn.Situation_Description: The Beyond indicates that the current situation is unlimited, it could destroy the universe, unmake anything, or change everything. It is an ultimate threat and often appears unstoppable, although usually there is a solution.
Yarn.Solution_Description: The Beyond indicates that the solution to the situation lies outside the current situation, this may mean a trip outside the affected area is required, or that the solution lies with some outsider who is new to the area, occasionally it means that no solution will be found without resorting to some kind of time-travel, parallel universes, or magical shenanigans.

Revelations.About_Description: Gods and magicians walk amongst us, at least in the Omniverse they do. The gods have great power, but are often limited in how they can apply it, magicians are not so restricted, but don't have as much power. Anyway this card allows you to reveal something about how these mages and gods are reacting to events in the Story.
Revelations.Info_Description: So there's something the Players would really want to know (like who is out to kill them, and why, and what they need to do about it, but then the Player's choose which one to know.
Revelations.Vector_Description: Direct Revelations are where the Villain monologues his plan to the captured hero, demonstrates his super-weapon to the United Nations, or shape-shifts in front of the Characters for the first time. Confessions, suicide notes, scientific papers, mission logs and journal and press conferences can all be Direct Revelations too. I'm sure you can think of others.
Revelations.Alternate_Description: The information contains the entire story, within the story (or play within the play), if anyone can see.
Revelations.Detail_Description: A random collection of lies and truths that weirdly seem to tie into each other.

Obstacle.Description: The way is blocked by some direct expression of the Beyond, it could be a Hellmouth, a portal into U-space, a gate to Shades, or a tear in space-time, or simply a dark spot full of Neechies (the least of the Increated), looking for inspiration and nightmare fuel to become perhaps even a new Patron.

Tarot.Normal: May be played as any Non-Court card Crossed (or as an Ordeal Card Joker with +22 Pips of Wyrd Penetration)
Tarot.Crossed: May be played as any Non-Court card

Ordeal.Stage_Description: The way ahead is a void, or huge open space. Maybe there's no cover, maybe not even air. Then again it could just be the space behind the walls, secret passages, or simply a way most people would never go.
Ordeal.Motional_Description: A Chase (or Flee) that goes around through the same Stages again.
Ordeal.Tide_of_Battle_Text: The Fight is taking place on open terrain, devoid of cover or natural advantages. Only a desperate fool would fight here by choice. Both sides would be best served by retreating now. +1 Pips to any Disengagement Action by either side.
Ordeal.Fight_Description:
The fight consists of waves of combatants. Each Round a new Wave begins, a Round ends automatically if all Combatants on one side are incapacitated. Typically each wave is of a different type, often beginning with Vex Monsters, rising in type and adding another Monster Facet, or doubling in number each Wave.
  Facet    Monster  
Awe ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Terror — The Monster is a Fear or Anxiety embodied. They are quite diverse in form, but are always able to cause Psych attacks and Negative Psych effects to any that see them act. They may add Awe Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cause negative emotions in another Character. They automatically draw and play Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) cards as a Psych Attack on any Character that sees them make any attack. They must take Awe Draw cards from their Monster Pool to defend, Soak or Heal any Attack made upon them. Examples of this Monster include Ghosts, some serial killers and some Preternatural Cosmic Horrors.
Burden ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Object — The Monster is an object imbued with the semblance of life. Artificial in nature usually, the living object is usually tough and strong. An Object Monster is created with a Monster Pool equal to their Burden Boon. Objects are able increase the Scores of any rolled die and Pips of all played cards by their Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) when appropriate to their form and function. The Object Monster may add Burden Score cards to it’s Monster Pool, if it makes, or has made, modifications, or upgrades to itself, usually using components it has secured. It may add Burden Draw cards to its Monster Pool any time it is mistaken for a normal Object, or is used in accordance to its form or function. The Object Monster must play cards from its Monster Pool to perform any action that is not a part of its normal form and function.
Craft ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Maker — The Monster is actually a creator of other Monsters. The spawn are often less powerful than the creator, but in some cases are unique and more powerful. The Creator may not be directly encountered until late in the Story. The Creator can draw Craft Draw cards and add them to its Monster Pool each time it creates or summons an Extra or Descendant. At least 1 card must be used from the Monster Pool each Summoning or Creation to help pay the Summoning costs (or as an Obstacle / NPC Yarn card). All currently summoned Extras and Descendants receive a bonus to their Annex Scores and card Pips equal to the Creator’s Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced).
Dominion ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Horde — The Monster is a group or swarm of lesser monsters, it might be an Army, a pack of Bulmäs, a Fortress or a Battle Moon. A Horde is effectively a Pact Descendant. A Horde may pool Values to create larger rolls, however they may never create a Boon greater than their individual Values. Hordes may add Dominion Draw cards to their Monster Pool when the Horde increases in number. The Horde must Play at least one card from their Monster Pool on any action that potentially splits or reduces the Horde (like losing an individual or being attacked). If the Horde Monster Pool is emptied the Horde is scattered as individuals again, and must reform to regain Monster Pool. The Horde automatically adds the current Monster Pool Size (Number of cards) to pay any costs for multiple targets and may also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Pips to any perception-based Actions. E.g. two Vex Extras of Boon 10 (Value 45) that form a Horde become a single Extra of Boon 16 (Value 90), (with probably 1-3 cards in the Monster Pool granting them a bonus to offset any costs from multiple targets and a smaller bonus to perception) but could never create a Boon greater than the individual’s Value(45). If there were 12, or One million, Vexes would give Boon 45 (Value 530) since that is the most powerful their Horde can get.
Enigma ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Skulker — The Skulker is a shadowy figure often pulling strings behind the scenes. This may just be an ordinary villain, but their ability to pass unnoticed should be bordering upon the unnatural. Perhaps they use psychic powers, or magic, to cloud the minds of witnesses, then again perhaps they are a hacking whiz, able to bounce phone calls through international relays with some multinational picking up the tab without realizing, or perhaps they are a Cryptid, an Alien Big Cat, Wolf man, Sasquatch or one of the Huldra, the hidden people - who maybe Fæ or the imaginary friend of one of the characters, possibly made real(ish) by an alien or an Increated.
Skulkers start with a Monster Pool of Enigma Score. Skulkers may add Enigma Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they hide, and if they hide completely for a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Enigma Boon they may add Enigma Score cards to their Monster Pool (e.g. Enigma Boon 12 grants 1 card when they hide, but if the Skulker hides until Dawn / Dusk / Noon or Midnight they may add 4 cards to the Pool).
Skulkers must Discard or Play half their Monster Pool any time they are spotted or found out. Often this will be spent on an immediate Hide action. They may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any stealthy, or hidden, actions or preparations. Skulkers that are hidden may play Monster Pool cards as Preparation to another’s actions as though they were making them. E.g. The Hacker can prepare a Hack attack, that is performed by a Church Website Server, in another town. A mind-bending villain could prepare a Psych Defence in the mind of his puppet. A Sasquatch might Prepare a Flee action on the hunter they have spotted in their territory, that they will trigger with a Fear emotional effect.
Fury ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Temperamental — The Monster is the embodiment of an Emotion, usually a negative one. Such Monsters are often capable of supernatural actions, such as telepathy, shape-shifting, or even undying. The Vampire is an embodiment of Lust, with powers of mind control, telepathy and suggestion, as well as undying and shape-shifting abilities. The Werewolf is embodiment of berserk rage, and is almost indestructible unless Silver is used. The Beansidhe is the embodiment of mourning, and able to sense an oncoming death. When they experience or induce their negative emotion the Monster may refill their Monster Pool to Fury Score cards. When affecting a Character who is afflicted with their emotion they may add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to their Actions. Temperamentals that are affected by emotional attacks must use cards from their Monster Pool to Ignore (Drawing equal to their Number of Active Hitches Reduced) the effect.
Gossamer ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Wiry — The Monster is immensely physically powerful, although often its form may not reveal it. It often demonstrates supernatural power as well as strength and toughness. A small, strong Goblin, a robot that looks like a little girl, a cybernetically enhanced rat, or even a killer doll (when not an Object Monster) are all examples of a Gossamer Monster. The Wiry Monster can add Gossamer Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever it is underestimated, cared for, or demonstrates its strength. A Wiry monster may add their current Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action with their strength or toughness. Wiries must spend cards from their Monster Pool as though it was a Prepared Defence to ignore damage.
Heresy ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Masker — The Monster is looks entirely like something else. Perhaps they are a Demon in the form of an innocent child, or maybe they are a shape-shifter or face-dancer, able to change their appearance as they like, then again perhaps they’re just the old Janitor in a monster costume. The Masker may add Heresy Score cards to their Monster Pool whenever they change or don their disguise (or mask) and Heresy Draw cards to the Monster Pool whenever their disguise works, or they are believed in their lies. The Monster Pool adds its Size Reduced Success Levels to any Action to do with hiding, maintaining the mask, or lying. The Masker must play at least one card from their Monster Pool any time their disguise is seen, examined, or observed as part of the action.
Inertia ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Unstoppable — The Monster is inexorable, it does not stop and cannot be killed by any normal means. They start play with a Monster Pool of Inertia Score cards. Unstoppable Monsters can add Inertia Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they cross a threshold, pass a barrier, or are forced to stop or turn around. Unstoppable Monsters can ignore a number of Wounds equal to the number of cards in their Monster Pool. Wounds stacked beyond the Monster Pool affect them as normal. Inexorable Monsters can also ignore all Hitches with a Boon less than their Monster Pool Size. Whenever an Unstoppable Monster is damaged beyond this limit, it must Discard the Highest Card in the Monster Pool, it may immediately also Discard any Wounds lower than discarded Monster Card, any remaining Wounds are then discarded on a one Monster Pool card per Wound basis. However, while discarding these wounds and cards the Monster must remain still (playing dead) until a Chi Extend Duration equal to the Number of Monster Pool cards Discarded (the initial card is instant, the next discard takes an action, then a second takes a few seconds, the third Wound takes an Ordeal Round, 25 Wounds would take a week). Examples of the Inertial Monster type include the Hungry Dead (Zombies) and most robotic assassins (especially ones from the future).
Jeer ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Mockery — The monster is a mockery — It might be illusionary, or Undead (usually a corpse animated by a low-level Increated), but it may be a ghost, a machine or even an animal that mocks something else. The Mockery is created with a Monster Pool of Jeer Score cards, that must be played to maintain the Mockery (when disbelieved, attacked, etc). While the Monster Pool exists the Monster can pull and play Monster Pool Size Reduced cards (initially Jeer Draw) for any action the Mockery form takes that it has no suitable Annexes for. The Mockery may add Jeer Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they are mistaken for what they mock. E.g. A Skeleton Mockery might have a "Mortality Reminder" Annex, but is given a Monster Pool of 5, so it can initially pull and play 2 cards for its mocking actions (like moving around and clawing the living). However, once attacked (or disbelieved) the Skeleton must spend at least one card from the Mockery Pool, thereafter it can only draw and Play 1 card for any Action that doesn’t involve reminding someone of their own Mortality. If the Skeleton is mistaken for a living being, then it may add 1 card to its Mockery Monster Pool.
Key ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Hunter — The Monster is a hunter or tracker, it should favour harrying attacks and hit and run tactics. Typically such a Monster will vanish or flee if it is wounded, only to reappear when healed. The hunter may be fooled by false trails, but never for long. Hunters may add Key Draw cards to their Monster Pool whenever they spot a track, clue, or similar, that indicates the subject of their hunt. Hunters receive a bonus to their Score or Pips equal to the number of cards in the Monster Pool on any tracking, attacking or hunting actions if they also spend a card from the Monster Pool on the action. Hunters may convert Mortal and Carnage Wounds that they take into Crippling Wounds instead, at a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. In T13, the archetypal version of this Monster is a Bulmäs Pack member from the Wild Hunt, but any Werewolf or Animal Shifter would probably qualify, as would robot cowboys in theme parks that have gone wrong, and any fantasy world’s Elven ranger.
Liberty ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Nightmare — The Monster is nightmarish, or dreamlike. Such Monsters either exist in a purely psychological realm, appearing as dreams and hallucinations, that cannot really be fought and don’t physically exist, or they are extremely dangerous and powerful Monsters that really exist and may be magical or supernatural in power. Nightmares may add Liberty Draw cards to their Monster Pool if they cause a Psych wound or appear in a dream. While they have cards in their Monster Pool they may add Liberty Score (usually rolled) to everything they do. The Nightmare may spend at least one card from their Monster Pool to do something physically impossible, they might use it to fly through the air, instantly teleport, let bullets pass through themselves, discard physical wounds, or appear in the waking world as examples. A Nightmare must discard a Monster Pool Card if they cross a threshold, barrier, or liminal space or time, or take a Psych Wound. Flower-faced Humanoids and Canines from a nightmare realm, handsome soap-sellers with an odd idea for gentlemen’s clubs, facially-burned, gardening-gloved janitors turned serial killers, six foot tall invisible rabbits, night hags, shadow beings seen when falling asleep, and any character in a dream, are all good examples of the Liberty Monster.
Miasma ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Mutant — The monster is a mutant or chimerical creature made from parts of others. This monster type is the oldest perhaps, you’ll find examples throughout the ancient world, particularly in Greek myth. The Mutant will often be larger, and may be malformed or unsymmetrical, with odd numbers of limbs, eyes, horns, or even heads. The Chimera will blend multiple animals together to create a new creature that combines features of all the “parent” creatures, preserving the best of all species. Mutant Monsters start with Miasma Score Monster Pool. They may add Miasma Draw cards to the Pool every time they accomplish a specified goal, such as eating a human, consuming a soul, bathing in radiation, or just growing a year older, etc. The Monster Pool can be used as a “Mutation” Annex, which can be used to mimic the abilities of the Mutants “parent” creatures, such as a spider’s ability to climb, or an eagle’s ability to fly. The Mutation Annex has a Boon equal to the Value of the Number of Cards in the Monster Pool, and so draws cards equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced and plays a number of cards equal to the Miasma Draw. The Mutation Annex is often used as a Size Annex for the Mutant. Where a 5 card Monster Pool would create a Boon 16 Annex, and could grant a Kaiju Mutant the size of a Skyscraper. 1950s B-movie monsters are often of this type, giant ants, lizards, spiders, wasps, and even rabbits have been used. Classically of course, Cyclopses, Centaurs, Minotaurs, Satyrs, Gryphons (or Griffins), Hippogriffs, Dragons, Sphinxes, Manticores and of course the Chimera itself are all examples. You can also find examples in superheroes, especially those created by radioactive arachnids, or those with an X-gene.
Nature ⚋ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Elemental — The Monster is an elemental force of nature, perhaps a literal elemental or more likely metaphorical. The Elemental will add Nature Draw cards to its Monster Pool when it encounters a natural source of its element, or when it causes collateral damage to a new location. Elementals gain +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Reduced and +1 Score or Pips per Monster Pool card, to any and all actions to do with their natural element. The Elemental must spend at least 1 card from its Monster Pool when soaking attacks that are not based upon its element. Salamanders, Sylphs, Gnomes, and Undines, Giants (of most types), are all example Elementals, as well as Forces-of-Nature and natural disasters such as storms as well as Tsunamis, landslides, pyroclastic flows and even blizzards (most of which might be Giants anyway).
Orthodox ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Tyrant — The Orthodox Monster is a tyrant, often a part of “the system” (in that it acts as a method of control over a population). A Tyrant monster add Orthodox Draw cards to their Monster Pool every time they are cruel, ruthless, authoritarian or demanding. Tyrants can add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels on another Character’s actions if they order them to do something. Tyrants can also add Monster Pool Size Score Orthodox Failure Levels to an enemy action for a cost of one card from their Monster Pool. The Tyrant must discard half of their their Monster Pool if they are ignored, forgotten or scorned.
Phoenix ⚊ ♥

Suit: Hearts ♥

Ordeal Trump: Healing (Survival / Magic) / Deception (Hide / Feint) / Magic (Positive)

Description: Hearts represent Love, Family and the comforts of home. Hearts are the warmth of a cosy fireplace, and social wealth rather than material. In the Tarot the suit is called Cups, and represents the feminine and elemental Water.

Tarot Meaning: Love, Relationships, Affections, Family, Sympathy, Peace.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Nurturing, Farming, Relationships, Medical. Hearts act as Trumps for the following Actions: Blending in, Cooking, Escaping, Healing, Hiding, Hunting, Insulting, Joke-telling, Lying, Magic, Mimicry, Riddling, Spinning a tale, Surgery, and Survival. Any type of Wound can be healed with a Heart.

Parry:Locked RespiteAll unmatched Cards from the original attack are Discarded by the Defender, however with each card they Discard they may either use the card to Heal/Stabilise one of their own wounds (or damage to the Parrying Descendant), or they may use the card to slow their attacker with additional RT (Pips) or GRT (Pips/5).

Soak: DisengageThe Soak Card acts as a Heal card, and is discarded along with the Attack card. This immediately Heals all Wounds in play that have less Pips, including the remaining Wounds in the Attack, by one Wound Level (discards Distracts).

Paragon — The Monster is the embodiment of a positive force or emotion, such as Love, Forgiveness, Warmth, Satisfaction or Wonder. Paragon Monsters are usually capable of causing positive (and sometimes negative) emotions via a Psych Attack in those that see them, and while they may be positive in nature, they are not necessarily good, or altruistic in their actions. The Paragon begins with a Monster Pool of cards equal to the Phoenix Score. The Paragon can discard half of their Monster Pool to multiply any Score by their Phoenix Score (usually rolled). The Paragon’s Monster Pool adds Phoenix Draw cards if the Paragon can find a source of their chosen emotion, and adds Phoenix Score cards if it can consume, interrupt or destroy this source (stopping a Character from feeling that emotion ever again would count as destroying the source, but so would eating them, changing their mood so they no longer feel that way woul be interrupting, as they could change back later). The Paragon must spend at least one card from its Monster Pool creating a Psych Attack for all observers whenever it is observed, which may be positive or Negative as the Monster / Yarn-Teller decides. The Phoenix itself is the embodiment of Resurrection, Hope and Faith, but is quite capable of burning destruction. The Golden Goose is another example, the embodiment of charity, but it evokes profound avarice in those who would own it. The unicorn is a symbol of purity, but is dangerous in the extreme to those it considers impure. Depictions of Angels are also perfect examples of positive embodiments.
Quiet ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Hush — The Quiet Monster is exactly that, almost (or entirely) silent. It is the creeping death of a hidden illness, or the cat-footed predator striking without warning that vanishes without trace. Hush Monsters get to begin with Quiet Score cards in their Monster Pool. Hushes may add +1 Score / Pip for each card in their Monster Pool when they try to act silently. When a Hush creature makes a noise (often a scream or howl, including a Psych attack), it must play at least half of the Monster Pool on the action. Hush Monsters may add cards to their Monster Pool based on how long it has been since the last time they made a noise as a Yarn Extend Duration (e.g. After being silent for a few seconds add a card, after a scene of silence add two. Be silent for a week and add 6 cards to the Pool). Creatures like giant owls, snakes or cats, that stalk and hunt silently are good examples, as is a really creepy, floating mime. Many ghosts are also silent, until they turn and scream to create a Duck attack.
Rook ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Guardian — The Monster is a Guardian, it guards a certain territory, path, treasure or person. Guardian Monsters may add Rook Draw to their Monster Pool whenever they interfere with another’s attack, or are attacked themselves. The Guardian will add Rook Score cards to its Monster Pool whenever they, or the thing they guard is Wounded or Damaged. A Guardian may spend a card from its Monster Pool to cancel another’s attack, this can be described as a dodge, or a soak. A Guardian may add a Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to any Attack or Defence action. From Smaug to Fafnir dragons are often Guardians, looking after a particular treasure or demanding tributes from a territory. Guardians often use area-denial Splash attacks, such as breath weapons, tail lashes, wing buffeted dust, venom sprays, gaze attacks, and explosions, to drive enemies from their territory.
Sin ⚊ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

Parry:DisarmAll the cards of the attack are immediately discarded, along with the Parry Card. The remaining cards of the Defender's Parry can now each be used to cause the Attacker to drop a Descendant (starting with the attacking weapon usually), can also cut armour straps, belts etc, or steal hats. This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Villain — The Sin Monster is an embodiment of a specific vice, taboo, or sin, often in the form of a human Villain (usually with animalistic features or tendencies). They usually have Annexes and Hitches that reflect their embodied vice or sin, and are often at least partially supernatural. Villains add Sin Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they, or another, indulges in their particular vice or behaviour. Which can be as broad as “Evil” or as focused as “Smokes Gauloises Cigarettes” as you like. Villains may add +1 Score / Pips for each card in their Monster Pool to any action that furthers their diabolical plans. Villains automatically spend Sin Draw Monster Pool cards on creating Obstacles when they escape or are threatened. The genocidal or narcissistic maniac, of the sort British Intelligence seems to tangle with repeatedly, could be a Sin Monster. The ever-hungry cannibal spirit, the Wendigo is definitely an example, as is the Sexual-predatory Lust-embodied Vampire.
Trial ⚊ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Killer — The Killer Monster is a deadly Combat specialist of some sort. Killers add Trial Draw cards to their Monster Pool each time they kill. Killers automatically add +1 Success Level per Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to all attacks, and Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) is automatically added to the Pips of any attack as Penetration. Killers must spend Monster Pool cards whenever they will make a Trick either with other cards being played by the Ordeal Pool, or other cards in the Round Pool or a Style Reserve. Killers can be drug-addled assassins, crazed shooters, or just steel-souled soldiers.
Virtue ⚋ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Tarot Meaning: Travel, Money, Business, Contracts, Deals, Voyages.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Travel (e.g. Chase, Race) / Creativity (e.g. Smithing, Painting). Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

Parry:Seize the initiativeThe cards of the original attack (less the matched card) are immediately added to the Defender’s Ordeal Pool. They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Friendly — The Monster is a “Friendly” monster, that will almost certainly be misunderstood and attacked by some people. They may add Virtue Draw cards to their Monster Pool by being kind to another, or whenever they are attacked. A friendly Monster protects their friends from harm, and must play cards from their Monster Pool to defend, protect, or heal them. Friendly monsters may add a number of Failure Levels equal to the Monster Pool Size Reduced to any enemy that tries to target them or their friends. Friendly Monsters can add Virtue Score (rolled Die or otherwise) to any positive action when playing a card from their Monster Pool. Examples might include a cynical, but big-hearted ogre; a being created from stitched together corpses; a dark-elf ranger with a unicorn necklace; or perhaps even an Electric Mouse.
Wyrd ⚋ ♣

Suit: Clubs ♣

Ordeal Trump: Defence / Trade / Control / Magic (Negative)

Description: Clubs represent the power of politics and a stoic, blind materialism. Clubs can be seen as a dark corrupting force on the world, crass materialism, sin, magic and anger, but the club is also symbolic of protection, law and justice. In the Tarot the Clubs are called Staves or Wands, in some traditions they represent Intellectual Air, but in most they are Fire.

Tarot Meaning: Power, Fame, Politics, Ability, Money.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Wealth, Politics, Power. Clubs act as Trump for following Actions: Bartering, Controlling, Corrupting, Emotional-intensity, Guarding, Magic, Displaying might, Politics, Self-Defence, Socializing, and Trade Negotiations. Clubs can be used to defend any type of attack.

This basically adds RT equal to the card Pips to the Annex that was used to attack. Every 5 Pips also creates a point of GRT for the Character (representing how much time the Character must spend picking up, or readying the weapon again. Court cards additionally may damage the Descendant that was disarmed, as standard for the Stakes.

Soak: WardThe matched Attack card, and one other randomly selected Attack card are discarded.

Doom-weaver — The monster is a creature of the Fæ, it can draw and play Yarn cards or Wyrd Tarot cards (as appropriate). The Wyrd Monster may play cards from its Monster Pool as Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards. The Monster may draw and add Wyrd Draw cards to its Monster Pool whenever it makes a choice, decision, or judgement. The Doom-weaver Monster may draw and Prepare Monster Pool Size Reduced cards whenever anyone else plays Wyrd Tarot or Yarn cards in the same Location. They must play a card from the Monster Pool if they have to undo a choice, decision or judgement they have made. Doom-Weavers come in many forms from the Cait-Sith, the Beansidhe, or even just powerful Mercari, to the Faery nobles of Ironmaster.
Yonder ⚋ ♠

Suit: Spades ♠

Ordeal Trump: Attack / Perception / Penetration (Opening / Navigation / Logic)

Description: The Spades Suit is the suit of suffering, said to be good for the soul, but miserable for a Monday. Competition, perception, endurance and curiosity are positive aspects associated with the suit, but spades also represent destruction, betrayal, distance, might and death. In the tarot, the Suit is replaced with Swords, which (depending on the tradition) may represent either damaging Fire, or more commonly the cutting intellect of Air.

Tarot Meaning: Misfortune, Suffering, Loss, Mourning, Treachery, Betrayal, War, Lawsuit, Conflict.

Ordeal Text: Ordeal Types: Confrontation (e.g. Warfare, Lawsuit), Competition (Sports, Quizzes etc), Perception (Spot Hidden). Spades act as Trumps for the Following Action Types: Attacking, Balance, Climbing, Contagion, Desecration, Destruction, Feats of Strength, Lock-picking, Navigation, Perception (Search/Spot), Staying power, and Stoicism. Spades can be used for any type of attack.

Parry:Redirect-AttackThe damage from the original attack (less the Parried Card) is redirected by the Defender to the Attacker, their Descendants or their Allies.

Soak: Counter-AttackThe matched Attack card is reflected back at the Attacker, or their Descendant, as Narratively appropriate.

Alien — The Monster is an alien, an outsider. It may just be a metaphor for alienation, or literally be an extra-(or ultra)-terrestrial. Such monsters are usually obvious and inhuman, not just to look at, but in the way they think and behave. They may have apparently supernatural powers and abilities, but often make do with advanced technologies (in the form of additional Descendants). They may add Yonder Draw cards to their Monster Pool when they are observed doing their alien / inhuman / weird actions. They may add Monster Pool Size Reduced Success Levels to any action to do with their alien abilities, technologies, motion or survival (including Dodge, Soak, etc). Alien Monsters must play cards from their Monster Pool to socialise with “non-aliens”. From little grey Eusaiveans, through pod people and pepper-pot shaped engines of hate, all the way to face-hugging space-wasps, there are plenty of aliens.
Zeal ⚊ ♦

Suit: Diamonds ♦

Ordeal Trump: Preparation (Research / Work / Patience) / Movement (Sneaking / Sprinting) / Listening

Description: Diamonds represent the finer things of life, they are bright and sparkling. Representing the creative even spiritual aspects of materialism such as beauty and art, that transcend base materialism like wealth. Despite this the Diamonds are recognised as being the Coins suit of the Tarot, with a strong tie to Elemental Earth.

Diamonds act as Trump for the following Action Types: Art, Building, Charming, Crafting, Driving, Emoting, Forgiving, Keeping Your Cool, Listening, Meditation, Motion, Prayer, Preparation, Research, Sprinting and Working in general. Any type of action can be prepared with a Diamond.

They may then play as many cards as their Descendant/Annex normally plays including the Parry card (1-4) usually into one of their Style Reserve, and then immediately discard any cards over Pool Limit.

Soak: ReadyThe matched Attack card is immediately Prepared by the Defender (as they wish). The Soak card is discarded.

Celestial — The Monster is a Celestial, a creature of divine or supernatural nature, often associated with a particular divinity, occasionally a political association, they often act as heralds of greater powers. Celestial beings include Anthropomorphic Personifications of social constructs like, Death, War, and Wyrd herself. When acting according to their nature, following their belief system, performing their duties, or following orders, they may draw Zeal Draw Ordeal cards into their Monster Pool. They automatically add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) Success Levels to any action to do with their nature, duty, travel, or communication. They also add Monster Pool Size Score (Number Reduced) to actions specific to their nature and duty. Celestial Monsters can only manifest a physical incarnation by playing cards directly from their Monster Pool on all physical actions. The monster could be an angel, a devil, a djinn, or perhaps even one of the Four Horsemen of the Apocalypse.
  Facet    Monster

Lea.Notes: The Grey path allows a Bulmäs Soul to sleep in the landscape. Their Soul may live in a skull, horn, tree or fossil for years, millennia or millions of years, until it is passed on, by ingestion of a fruit or nut, or by a Soul-Release ceremony. Fossil Bulmäs include Moon-Staves, Flint Weapons (Axes or Knives) and Elf-shot Armour that can only be worn / controlled by a Bulmäs who have a Soul related to the Fossil Soul.

Stress.Description: Stress can break a Character, ruining their tales, derailing their arcs and upsetting Plots that have Embodied in them. Then again sometimes the Plots that are working behind the Scenes can decide to intervene and keep a Character on track, throwing some narrative event at the Character that forces them to confront their own faults and deal with their Stress.
Stress.Rules: The Character immediately spends all current Stress increasing the Tension Level of their personal Arc one Level. This will usually bring on a Scene from that Plot that plays out following the the current Scene directly.

Trauma.Description: Trauma can change a Character in really vital ways, like their vitality, this Trauma kills them.
Trauma.Rules: The Trauma immediately Relieves all Stress into Chi. Then the Character "dies", releasing all their Sway as a Yarn-burst, and then moves to another layer of reality, or alters their Incarna as required (See Death and Immortality Rules).

Age.Description: An Age of Incursion is occurring. During this age a society is infiltrated from beyond, usually this will be simply some foreign power, but in some genres this may be a cross-temporal / hyper-geometric / inter-dimensional / extraterrestrial / ultraterrestrial / supernatural / spiritual / divine / demonic or Increated incursion. The Incursion can be an expedition, infiltration, intrusion, raid, abduction, invasion, usurpation, or just a vacation.
Age.Rules: During an Age of Incursion some force from beyond, either the society, nature or even the genre, appears within the society. This incursion can be for any purpose, but represents some exterior unknowable force. When in doubt use any crazy idea, you can always say it was an Increated that nightmare fueled off a nearby insane person later, if necessaary. Incursions can bring changes to not just the society, but the nature of reality itself, as time-travellers, gods or sleeping dragons awaken and emerge into the society.
